key_insight: LMV-Net combines longitudinal mammography with complementary CC and MLO views to improve breast cancer risk prediction.
clinical_relevance: Better risk stratification could support more personalized screening intervals and earlier identification of higher-risk patients.
method: Jointly analyzes CC and MLO mammographic views
Uses explicit longitudinal alignment across screening exams
Benchmarked on the public EMBED and CSAW-CC datasets against state-of-the-art methods
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent performance gains over existing risk prediction methods, including across breast density and cancer subgroups, on EMBED and CSAW-CC.
watch_item: As a screening-risk model, real-world impact will depend on external validation, integration into clinical workflows, and careful calibration across sites and populations.
paper_title: Longitudinal Multi-View Breast Cancer Risk Prediction
